title: BridgeFlux Support Systems
preview:
Lead source: inbound via product signup (self-serve). Company builds "FluxAgent" — an agent layer for small-mid market support teams. Wants to integrate Redwood hosted API during a 10-day POC.

Key requirements (verbatim & shorthand captured):
- Workloads: high volume short-text ticket summarization + structured JSON outputs for CRM ingestion. Also direct tool-calls to update ticket status in their system (webhook-based).
- Latency/throughput: aiming for ~150-250ms p95 for single-turn summary calls; expected concurrency 5-15 qps during peak; bursty behavior from shared inbox.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB — "cost per agent" matters; wants numbers for 7B quantized vs 13B hosted variants. Asked: "Can we run cheap open models for summaries and fallback to higher-quality for escalations?"
- Model preference: open-model friendly; prefers hosted open models in Redwood catalog (7B quantized for majority, 13B for fallback). Willing to try Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching.
- Structured output: strict JSON schema required for their CRM (fields: summary_short, summary_long, top_tags[], action_items[]). Needs example schema and Redwood's structured output reliability guidance.
- Tool calling: must be able to invoke a ticket-update webhook with authentication; needs SDK examples for node/python. Wants sample function-call patterns for idempotent updates and rollback on failure.
- Observability: wants per-route latency and per-token cost breakdown in Console. Need retention of logs for 30 days.
- Security: requires SSO/SAML for team, SOC2 attestation, audit logs; data residency not strict (US preferred).

POC scope agreed: 10 days hosted API POC — endpoints: /summarize (streaming), /structured (JSON), /tool-call (secure webhook). Goals: demonstrate <250ms p95 for summaries, structured output accuracy >90 on curated prompt set, and safe tool-call retries.

Conversation snippets:
- "We love the quick sign-up — want to see how it fits into our FluxAgent flow before committing." — VP Product
- "If we can get reliable JSON output and cheap per-agent costs we'll expand to 150 agents after POC." — Head of Ops

Stubs/attachments: pricing deck path (drive), security Q uploaded (SUPPORT-1123). Gmail thread contains initial self-serve signup logs. Fireflies transcript ff-20260215 includes call timestamps and action items.

Internal notes: good PLG motion — engaged engineering team, receptive to Optimize recommendations. Primary risk: cost sensitivity and SOC2 ask (we should surface SOC2 letter + quickstart for hosted API). SE to prepare example toolbox with batching/quantization tradeoffs and sample Node/Python snippets for function-calling webhook flow.

Next steps (action items):
- Maya: send sandbox API creds + quickstart + tailored pricing sketch (today).
- Ethan: prepare POC checklist and fidelity targets; include sample JSON schema tests and streaming example (due 2026-03-15).
- Follow-up demo: schedule 30m POC walkthrough week of 2026-03-16.

title: Brighton Helix Chat
preview:
Inbound SMB demo lead from Product Hunt link — lightweight B2B chat SDK maker. Early-stage (pre-series A), 60 engineers+product. Want to embed a streaming assistant into their product (in-app chat + contextual suggestions). Key asks: low token-latency (p50 ~100ms), streaming partial responses to keep UX snappy, configurable fallback routing when quota hits. Prefer open/compatible model flavors (quantized Llama variants or other open LLMs) but will evaluate Redwood hosted first for speed. Cost sensitive — expects ~5k-20k ARR initially from hosted API usage; wants clear per-token breakdown.

Sales notes (shorthand):
- "We need to make the assistant feel instantaneous" — Priya (Dev Lead)
- Will demo in-app flow using small client app next week.
- Trial org already has API keys, devs started integration (websocket + streaming). Observed ~140-180ms p50 in initial tests (non-optimized). Wants to see kernel/runtime tuning + batching suggestions.
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO later; retention 30d default ok but may request audit logs for enterprise later.
- Routing/fallback: must be able to route to cheaper model variant on quota exhaustion w/ graceful degradation of responses.

POC ask: 48-hour performance run on hosted_api with 1k parallel short sessions, streaming enabled; capture p50/p90 latency and token cost per convo. SE to provide perf checklist and sample load script. EA to secure buy-in from VP Product.

title: Ceramic Orchard Helpdesk
preview:
Quick inbound trial signup (self-serve) — CRM lead from organic blog post on quick prompt guides.

Firmographics: SMB helpdesk SaaS, ~120 employees, primarily NA HQ, 30k monthly active support conversations. Looking to add conversational assistant and fast summarization for agent inbox.

Initial ask: "We signed up and love the simplicity. Production concern: responses feel slow for EU customers — we see 800-1,200 ms extra lag compared to US. Is there an easy region config?" — spoken by Head of Product on 2026-03-05 demo call.

What we've done so far (timeline):
- 2026-02-25: Self-serve signup, free credit used.
- 2026-03-02: Auto-instrumented latency metrics show p50 p95 differences by region; user reported EU p95 ~1.8s vs US p95 ~650ms.
- 2026-03-05: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260305_8923). Demo of hosted_api, basic SDK usage.
- 2026-03-08: Customer tried switching region param in SDK to eu-west-1; saw minor improvement but still high tail latency. Provided quick checklist (use short prompts, enable streaming, reduce max_tokens).
- 2026-03-10: Raised INF-5123 for deeper investigation; engineering performed a synthetic run and flagged prompt length and batching misconfiguration.

Technical profile & requirements: 
- Primary workload: short chat-turns for agent assist (single-turn + context), and ticket summarization (concise outputs).
- Latency targets: p95 < 900ms for chat, p95 < 2s for summarization in EU.
- Throughput: bursty — 7–20 rps during business hours per tenant. Cost sensitivity: moderate — prefers hosted usage but wants configuration to reduce token costs.
- Model preference: open/verified mid-size models acceptable if latency/cost tradeoff documented.
- Routing/fallback: prefer region-aware routing to nearest data center; fallback to US region only if explicitly allowed.

Observed issues and hypotheses: 
- Calls from EU routed to US by default in their SDK usage; region param not persisted in client.
- Customers sending long system prompts + full conversation history on every request (no prefix caching).
- SDK default timeout is low for some EU network conditions; some client-side retries amplify tail latency.

Immediate recommendations provided to customer: 
1) Verify region parameter is set at client init and not per-request. Example snippet shared in call notes (drive:1Perf-Playbook).
2) Shorten system prompts and move stable context to prefix cache in app layer.
3) Enable streaming to reduce TTFB for first tokens; reduce max_tokens for summarization job (chunk long tickets).
4) Increase client-side read timeout to 10s and add jittered retry policy (1 retry) to avoid thundering retries.
5) Consider model profile with lower latency (quantized mid-size) — SE to share recommended list and perf numbers.

Customer quotes and tone: 
- "We need sub-second perceived response for agents — anything slower kills adoption." — Head of Product.
- Tone: pragmatic, open to tuning, but needs quick wins before moving to paid tier.

Next steps (owned):
- SE (Maya) to run an EU synthetic test and produce p50/p95 matrix for three model profiles (due 2026-03-16).
- Schedule 60m perf tuning workshop w/ eng + product (propose 2026-03-18).
- Send short SDK snippet and region-check checklist (done, linked).

Potential blockers: finance signoff, team bandwidth to implement caching, and verifying that EU data residency/latency acceptable under hosted offering.

CRM shorthand: discovery -> high intent but needs perf fixes. Keep close, two-week cadence for follow-ups.

title: DevReview Ally
preview:
-- Company background:
- Seeded in 2020, builds a GitHub-style PR review assistant used by internal engineering and SRE teams.
- Focus: automated diff summarization, actionable comment generation, reranking candidate comments based on code context and style guides.

-- Primary ask for Redwood:
- Low-latency token-streaming inside IDE (VS Code extension) for near-real-time comment suggestion (target p95 < 100ms for token chunks).
- Deterministic PR summaries for CI checks (model/version pinning + reroll strategy).
- Reranking service for top-3 comment candidates, with adjustable scoring weights (security, style, performance).
- Support for multi-model routing: small fast model for short diffs, larger model for complex refactors.

-- Security & deployment constraints:
- Must run within VPC for customer repos that contain PII/credentials; enterprise customers require private deployment option.
- SSO via SAML, audit logging retained 1 year, KMS-managed keys for encryption, SOC2 compliance required.
- Data residency: EU customers require EU-only hosting.

-- Timeline / recent activities:
- 2026-02-11: Kickoff call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-11_DevReviewKickoff). AE Maya + SE Ethan joined. Demo of competitor Copilot integration.
- 2026-02-18: Sent initial security questionnaire (drive:/deals/devreview/pr-technical-reqs.md).
- 2026-03-02: POC checkpoint call (Fireflies ff_2026-03-02_POC-Checkpoint). Confirmed GitHub App webhook flow and sample repo.
- 2026-03-05: Engineering asked for sample latency traces and kernel selection notes (email thread_devreview_sales_20260210).
- POC milestones (customer-specified):
  * Week 1: GitHub App + VS Code extension integration, basic streaming enabled.
  * Week 2: Diff summarization accuracy run on 500 repos, measure F1 vs human summaries.
  * Week 3: Security review and penetration checklist (legal + infra).
  * Week 4: Scale test (200 concurrent reviewers, 300 req/s sustained) and cost estimate.

-- Technical preferences / details captured:
- Preferred model types: quantized LLaMA-family or Llama2-variants for balance of latency and quality. Customer asked about Redwood's verified model catalog and recommended quantization profiles.
- Model pinning required per-repo and per-branch (must support rollout/canary to 5% of traffic).
- Caching: aggressive prefix/KV cache for long-running threads; needs hinting APIs for repo-specific caches.
- Reranking: wants ability to plug in custom heuristics (security scanner scores) into rerank pipeline.

-- Quote from Eng Lead (paraphrased): "We need deterministic outputs for CI commentary — if the bot comments wrongly it blocks merges. Also want a fast fallback path so reviewers aren't blocked if model is busy."

-- Commercial notes:
- Interested in Dedicated for predictable latency with the option to move to Private for EU-only enterprise customers.
- Budget: internal estimate $50k-150k ARR depending on scale and retention.

-- Next steps / ask list:
- Provide Dedicated trial access and a sandboxed GitHub App for POC.
- Security deep-dive: SOC2 report + KMS integration docs.
- Latency benchmark runbook and recommended kernel/quantization settings for their workload.
- Legal to review data residency clause; request sample contract language.

-- Internal AE notes:
- AE Maya to coordinate legal doc handoff. SE Ethan to run two targeted latency tests week of 2026-03-15. Follow-up scheduled 2026-03-18.

title: Halo Care Cooperative
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from wellness network. Wants to use Redwood Hosted API for: intake form parsing -> summarize -> populate internal CRM + support bot for members. HIPAA-adjacent — not full covered entity but handles sensitive PII/PHI-like fields. Key quotes: "We need to be 90% sure PHI never enters logs / long-term storage" and "Is hosted an option if we run a de-id step client-side?". 
Short summary: customer prefers low-friction hosted route (self-serve), cost-sensitive, wants guidance on retention and minimal logs. Interested in token-level cost breakdowns and examples of prefix/KV caching to reduce cost. SE call covered: sdk, streaming, how to implement client-side de-id (strip names, DOB, MRNs) before send, pros/cons. Customer concerns: server-side logs (access/audit), backups, S3/object retention for attachments. Legal wants documented retention SLA and whether request/response bodies are accessible to platform engineers.
Action items (AE/SE): send tailored retention + de-id playbook, sample code snippet for client-side de-identification, patch of hosted terms re: access to request data, schedule 30m security call wk of 3/18.
Stubs/links: demo recording (ff_20260302_1423_01), proposal deck (drive:/decks/halo-hosted-api-q1-proposal.pdf), retention faq (drive:/security/retention-pricing-cheatsheet.pdf), legal ticket SEC-2109.
Notes style: fragments and follow-ups — will likely convert to pilot (3mo) if legal sign-off OK. Budget: small (5k-20k ARR).

title: Auraborne VelocityWorks
preview:
Summary: Auraborne is an adtech SSP/RTB stack (header bidding + real-time personalization). They need sub-50ms p95 end-to-end inference for impression-time scoring and reranking, with peak bursts to ~6k rps and sustained 1.2k rps. Very cost-sensitive: want to evaluate batching, KV/prefix caching, and aggressive quantization to reduce GPU count and TCO.

Key requirements (from AE/SE calls):
- p95 <= 50ms for scoring path; target 30ms for experiments.
- Throughput: baseline 1.2k rps, burstable to 6k rps for 2-5s windows.
- Deterministic fallbacks: fallback routing to smaller quantized model when Dedicated pool saturated.
- Cold-start behavior: minimal latency for first request after idle (we discussed warm pools + micro-batching).
- Model preferences: want to test Llama2-13B-quant, Mistral-7B-8bit, and Redwood-verified 7B variants.
- Data constraints: ad payloads include user IDs (hashed). Must remain in customer VPC; logs retention 90d; require KMS integration + audit log export.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-01-20: Initial inbound demo request via website (AE: Maya assigned).
- 2026-02-03: Intro call (Maya/Ethan) — discovery; high-level perf targets captured. (ff_20260203_auraborne_intro)
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire submitted; SEC ticket opened (SEC-451).

title: AxiomFlux RAGServe
preview:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (customer support KB) evaluating Redwood hosted API for an embeddings + ANN frontend and cross-encoder reranker. Priority = high QPS + predictable cost.

Context and needs:
- Indexing scale: ~1.0-1.4M canonical docs (mix of articles, transcripts), ~20M embedding vectors expected after passage-splitting. Daily delta ~3-5k docs.
- Query profile: customer-facing help widget + internal agent assist. Peak QPS target 500-650 qps, baseline ~250-350 qps. 95th-tail SLA request: sub-150ms response for retrieval-only; end-to-end (retrieval + rerank) target <350ms.
- Cost sensitivity: want working cost model. Ballpark target <$0.001 per served user query (prefer ~ $0.0005 - $0.0008) including embedding retrieval + rerank.
- Quality: first-pass ANN recall top-100 then cross-encoder rerank for final top-5. Need support for dense retrieval + on-the-fly reranking.
- Feature asks: incremental index updates, TTL/freshness tuning, partial residency (EU subset), request-level audit logging, SSO (SAML) for admin console, KMS integration for keys.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (AE Ethan) — high-level product fit, asked for rough perf numbers and pricing.
- 2025-12-05: Architecture deep-dive with SE (Rina) — validated pipeline idea (ANN -> cross-encoder). Action: Redwood to propose POC config.
- 2026-01-18: Security screening kickoff — customer provided compliance checklist. Legal: wants SOC2 in writing + data residency options.
- 2026-02-04: Internal sizing workshop — AxiomFlux estimated 1.2M docs; Rina suggested batching + prefix caching.
- 2026-03-03: Demo of hosted API (small sample) — customer liked latency and SDKs but asked for scaled run. Fireflies: ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting.
- 2026-03-10: Pricing scoping call — customer requested a cost model for 300 qps and for a 600 qps bursting scenario.

POC ask / success criteria:
- Redwood runs a perf POC on hosted API using a representative 1M-doc index (customer uploads sanitized sample).
- Measure: retrieval-only (ANN) 95th < 150ms at 500 qps; retrieval+rerank (top-100 -> cross-encoder) end-to-end <350ms at 300 qps.
- Cost calc delivered: $/query breakdown (embeddings + ANN + reranker) and optimization recommendations (quantization, batching, cache TTLs).

Customer quotes and sentiment:
- "We need the widget to feel instant for customers but can move heavy reranking to agent side if tails don't behave."
- Product lead: "If you can show <0.8ms/k token cost improvement w/ caching & batching we'll move forward." (paraphrase)

Technical notes / constraints:
- Preferred models: open-weights embedding model + a cross-encoder for rerank (customer open to Redwood model recommendations).
- They prefer to keep documents indexed in public cloud (AWS) but require EU residency for EU-customer content.
- PII scrub pipeline planned; legal will require proof of audit logging and retention controls.

Next steps (short):
1) Schedule hosted API perf POC run window (Rina + infra team) — target week of 2026-03-24.
2) Send tailored pricing deck + breakdown by component (embeddings, ANN, rerank). Link: /drive/pricing/axiomflux-pricing-deck-v2.pdf.
3) Respond to security checklist w/ SOC2 evidence and KMS integration notes — attach security FAQ (https://drive.redwood.com/security/axiomflux-security-faq.pdf).

Open blockers (detail):
- Legal: confirmation of SOC2 scope and timeline for attestation (customer legal requires before signing).
- Residency: need a plan for partial EU-only index + confirm data egress controls.
- Perf/cost proof: customer wants measured POC results at 1M docs at representative QPS before committing to dedicated capacity.

Notes on engagement tone: ongoing, pragmatic. Customer likes engineering-first conversations. Not price-led only; performance and controls are decision drivers.

Attachments & refs: pricing deck, security FAQ, Fireflies transcript id ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting, Jira RWD-11234 (request to scale test infra).

title: Blue Harbor Integrations
preview:
Lead: Blue Harbor Integrations — small dev tools shop building a docs-first developer portal. Primary ask: quick, drop-in examples to wire Redwood hosted API into their existing Next.js site using Vercel AI SDK and LangChain/LlamaIndex for retrieval. CTO quote: "We want a one-page quickstart to drop into Next.js and a copy-paste adapter for LangChain/LlamaIndex." Shorthand/CRM bullets:
- Using Next.js 14 + Vercel platform (deploys on Vercel).
- POC scope: connect docs (S3-hosted markdown) -> embeddings -> semantic search -> chat with context.
- Wants examples for both LangChain (agents + chains) and LlamaIndex (index + query) that show how to call Redwood hosted API with streaming and structured output.
- Concerned about cold-start latency and median token latency <150ms if possible; willing to accept bursty throughput.
- Billing: wants clear mapping of tokens -> estimated monthly cost for ~200k tokens/mo.
- Security: needs SOC2 mention, SSO later (not required for POC).
- Quote on demo: "If we can get a working quickstart in 2 dev days we'll push to staging."

title: Catalyst Haven Systems
preview:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics) — looking to replace slow in-house reranker and move support agent → semantic doc-search. Started on Redwood Hosted API; results promising for recall but unit cost and rate limits are a concern for scaled product usage. Team prefers to evaluate Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) to get predictable throughput and lower per-token costs.

Key POC asks / technical requirements:
- Workload: hybrid (real-time chat/support-agent latency sensitive ~ < 300ms p95; batch reranking for search results at 200 qps peak).
- Model preference: prefer curated open models; want a quantized 13B/7B variant option (Llama-like 13B-quant / 7B-quant) for cost-performance tradeoffs.
- Routing/fallback: POC must include canary rollout (5% -> 25% -> 100%), plus automatic fallback to Hosted API for overflow or regional outage. They want documented rollback triggers (latency > 350ms p95, error rate > 1% or quality regression on eval set).
- Regions: primary US-West, failover EU-West for users in EMEA, data residency for EU customers must stay in EU region.
- Security: SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration for keys, retention controls (30/90 day retention policies).
- Networking: VPC peering required for Private control plane; egress restrictions in some customer tenants.
- Observability: token-level cost breakdown, per-route latency, and sample outputs for QA.

POC plan (agreed):
1) Week 0: infra prep — provision Dedicated pool (2xA10 equivalent), VPC peering, service account and KMS test.
2) Week 1: Deploy model variants (13B-quant as primary, 7B-quant as cheap fallback), enable KV prefix cache for support flows.
3) Week 2: Canary traffic (5%) routed from production via feature flag; routing policy: route based on tenant and latency SLO preference. Collect eval metrics + human eval on sample prompts.
4) Week 3: Increase canary (25%), run cost/perf analysis and run rollback tests (simulated GPU outage) — verify region failover to EU and fallback to Hosted API.
5) Week 4: Finalize SLOs, document runbook for rollback and capacity alerts, pricing/commercial term negotiation.

Stated concerns from Eng/Product: "We need deterministic cost numbers before committing"; "If the Dedicated pool degrades, must not break customer-facing chat - fallback must be seamless".

Artifacts / references: Fireflies call FF-12903 (design session), pricing deck in Drive, security questionnaire (link below). SE notes: Lina to provide canary routing example policy and a JSON of rollback thresholds.

preview:

Evaluating Redwood hosted API for quick embed generation + semantic search integration, then Dedicated for predictable throughput and VPC. Key ask: scale nightly batch embedding of ~150k docs and support 200 QPS vector lookups with <80ms median retrieval. Prefers open model families (Llama2/compatible) and quantized 8-bit variants; wants automatic fallback to smaller model during peak hours.

Sales/POC notes: demo 2026-03-03 showed Hosted API latencies acceptable for dev traffic; concerns around token costs for embeddings at scale. title: Aurora Bridge Search
preview:
Shorthand notes from calls and threads:
- Company: Aurora Bridge Search, small legaltech startup building client-facing contract search + internal research.
- Primary ask: replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranking pipeline; want hosted API (self-serve) to move fast.
- Latency target: interactive search ~<300ms (p95) on 1-10 qps baseline; may scale to 30-50 qps during peak. Cost sensitive — want guidance on tokens vs embedding costs.
- Model preference: open to small/efficient text-embedding models; asked about support for OpenAI-style embeddings + local quantized alternatives.
- Vector DB question: currently evaluating Pinecone and Postgres (pgvector); asked whether Redwood hosted API has first-class connectors or recommended adapter patterns.
- Reranking: customer expects two-stage pipeline: ANN candidate retrieval -> rerank with cross-encoder/generation signal. Wants examples for combining Redwood generative scoring with embeddings.
- Data handling: public cloud ok, no air-gap. Asked about audit logs for queries (legal compliance) and SSO for admin console.
- Quote from CTO: "We need something that just works without ops overhead — self-serve to start, but must be production-ready if we grow."
- Action items: send short how-to for Pinecone + Redwood embeddings, provide cost estimate for 10k doc POC, sample reranking prompt templates, and link to hosted API quickstart.
- Risk: budget approval depends on Q2 hiring; security review expected to be light but must show audit logs and basic retention controls.
- Competitive signals: they trialed Pinecone + Hugging Face embeddings last month, hit cost/latency tradeoffs and manual rerank complexity. Open to switching if integration simpler.

title: IndiePrompt Labs
preview:
Origin: indie dev founder (solo) building VSCode plugin for code completions and small chat flows. Self-serve signup + quick trial.

Highlights and quotes:
- 'Need something cheap and predictable — we're price sensitive, monthly budget ~$50-200 to start.'
- 'Latency under ~300ms ideally for interactive editor experience; can tolerate <200rps bursts.'

Technical/reqs (from call + trial logs):
- Primary workload: code-assistant (light context, streaming responses), chat for assistant-style replies, summarization for README files.
- Target p99 latency: <=300ms for short prompts; typical token counts small (10-150).
- Throughput: very low to start (single-digit concurrent requests), may scale to ~20-30 weekly active users in next 3 months.
- Cost sensitivity: high. Asked about batching/quantization recommendations; interested in Optimize suggestions but wants self-serve controls.
- Model preference: open models (cheaper) — asked about Redwood model catalog & streaming; will try small Llama variants or other cost-conscious models.
- Deployment: public cloud only; no VPC/on-prem. No SSO required at this stage.
- Security: wants basic audit_logging + token rotation best practices. No SOC2/ISO need yet.

Engagement notes / behavioral signals:
- Using Node SDK, repo samples from docs used as-is.
- Reached out via support chat asking about per-token math and how to limit hallucinations.
- Liked the streaming examples; asked whether function calling helps with structured completions for completions->AST flow.

Next steps / playbook items (CRM):
- Send tailored one-pager showing cost estimates for small vs medium usage (drive link).
- Offer community invite (Slack) and encourage to file simple prompt-evals to track regressions.
- Monitor usage for quota bump/upgrade triggers; set alert to check in if spend > $30/week.

title: BlueHarbor Goodsight
preview:
Summary: BlueHarbor Goodsight runs a large e-comm catalog (approx 30M SKUs) and is evaluating a 2-stage semantic search + rerank pipeline to improve discoverability and conversion. Primary focus is embeddings for candidate retrieval (dense vector index) + a lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-10. Current infra is TF-IDF + heuristic rules; business wants better semantic recall without blowing up cost.

POC scope: 2-week pilot indexing 2M representative SKUs, simulate 1M q/day load, measure recall (NDCG), latency, and cost per query. They provided a product feed sample and session traces. Beta metrics target: candidate retrieval p95 < 120ms, rerank end-to-end (top-10) p95 < 200ms. Cost target: combined embedding+rerank budget $0.0006 - $0.001 per query at baseline; acceptable tradeoffs for short promo windows but need predictable ceilings for Black Friday (expected 6-8x baseline).

Security & ops: Require VPC deployment, SSO (SAML), KMS key management, audit logs and US data residency for payment/account data. Enterprise procurement will require an SOC2 report and retention controls.

Model preference & optimization asks: prefer CPU-friendly/quantized vector models for retrieval and a distilled cross-encoder for ranking; want Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching, quantization profiles, and KV caching to hit cost/SLO targets. Interested in automated fallback policies (cheaper model variant) during bursts.

Quotes / notes from last call: "If we can't get predictable cost during BF spikes we won't move off current infra." - Laura Morales (Head of Search). AE follow-up: send detailed cost model and example rollout for canary -> global.

title: CopperFalcon Insights
preview:
Customer: mid-market SaaS for customer support analytics. Current search stack: BM25 + embeddings (in-house). Pain: poor precision at top-k causing low deflection and higher agent workload. Evaluation path: hosted API first -> POC offline rerank on 50k queries -> small online A/B -> pricing discussion for dedicated if cost/throughput warrants.

POC details and asks:
- They want to use our rerank endpoint (offline scoring CSV) for head-to-head with their current ranker.
- Need clear instructions for eval set formatting (we provided template).
- Latency constraint: 95th pctl for single rerank RPC <200ms; acceptable to batch for bulk offline scoring.
- Cost sensitivity: targeting <$0.20 per user-active-month equivalent; looking to model unit economics for hosted vs dedicated.
- Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller model in case of capacity spike; also region-aware routing (US primary, EU data residency for some customers).

Security/compliance:
- VPC preferred, SOC2 required, SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS key management. Data residency: EU for certain enterprise customers.

Quotes / paraphrases:
- PM: 'We need a measurable bump in precision@5 without blowing up latency or cost.'
- Eng lead: 'If dedicated yields predictable throughput and cost parity, we'll seriously consider committed capacity.'

Next tactical items (short):
- SE to share dedicated pricing sheet + committed-capacity scenarios (by 2026-03-12).
- AE to coordinate legal for DPA and KMS questions.
- SE running extra offline runs to produce recall/precision curves per model variant (embedding + rerank model combos).

Notes: customer likes the offline scoring workflow; wants clearer documentation on canary A/B and rollback for Dedicated. Important to capture and surface token-level cost for rerank flows in Redwood Console for their finance team.

title: CounselMesh Knowledge Hub
preview:
Summary: CounselMesh is a mid-size e-discovery and compliance provider moving from a homegrown Elastic+vector plugin to a vendor-managed RAG stack. They are heavy on retention/audit requirements and want a private/VPC deployment with customer-managed keys.

Conversation highlights:
- \"We need verifiable audit trails for every query and admin action; retention policies must be enforceable per-case.\" (Head of Security)
- High indexing scale: currently ~120M documents (mixed PDFs, native docs, audio transcripts). Estimated growth to 250M over 18 months.
- Expected query profile: 30-50k embedding lookups/day, peak concurrent rerank pipelines ~600 qps. Overall latency SLO: <= 200ms for 1-to-5 candidate rerank path for top-5 results.
- Cost sensitivity: prefer cost-per-query model for embeddings+rerank, want tooling to project unit costs for different batching/quantization profiles.

What they liked about Redwood:
- Unified private control plane option + clear audit log expectations.
- Optimize recommendations (batching/quantization) to reduce cost without sacrificing recall.

Open items / asks:
- Security questionnaire (SaaS + private) answered and returned by 2026-03-15.
- Demo environment in customer VPC or private pilot with sample index (50k docs) for performance validation.
- Detailed cost model: embeddings storage + ANN index ops + reranking compute per 1M queries.

AE notes (Maya):
- Pricing will likely be a 12-month committed Dedicated+Optimize bundle. Push to close by May 2026.
- Legal already signaled strict data residency clauses; need legal ops to pre-approve our MSA redlines.

Staged POC plan (high level):
1) Security review & network onboarding (VPN cert exchange)
2) Pilot: 50k-doc index + baseline embeddings/rerank (2 weeks)
3) Scale test: simulate 600 qps rerank + 50k qps embedding lookups (expand as infra allows)
4) Cost/quality evaluation & contract signature.

title: Helio SprintLabs Copilot
preview:
Summary: Helio SprintLabs is building an IDE copilot integrated into their enterprise IDE that emphasizes near-instant streaming completions for developer UX. Primary ask: consistently fast Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) for completion streaming + predictable token budget behavior so editor doesn't 'blink' or overload the language model.

Recent customer feedback / paraphrased quotes:
- "TTFT must feel instant — target <80ms for first token on warm paths; jitter must be minimized (<10ms ideally)" — Head of Platform
- "We need backpressure in the editor: if generation stalls or token budget near-exhausted, return a graceful partial completion and a stable fallback" — Lead UX Engineer

Technical requirements (from calls):
- Primary workload: low-latency code completion streaming (single-line and multi-line completions), small-context code chat for quick lookups.
- Latency targets: TTFT <80ms warm, <150ms cold-first (acceptable if KV-cache warmup unavoidable).
- Jitter: p95 jitter <12ms; p99 <30ms.
- Throughput: per-customer peak 120 concurrent live sessions, average completion length 40–120 tokens.
- Token budget model: per-session soft budget 1500 tokens/day with priority decay; need hooks to signal client when 75% and 90% thresholds reached.
- Backpressure behavior: prefer server-driven pacing (stream chunk sizes) + an immediate ability to send "partial final" tokens if connectivity drops.
- Model/version pinning: must pin to a verified low-latency variant (requested: "mosaic-lite-latopt-v2" as primary) with automated fallback to smaller quantized variant for overflow.
- Caching: warm KV cache strategies, prefetch top-N token prefixes for hot repo contexts.
- Autoscale policy: hybrid burst to hosted for spikes but prefer Dedicated for steady throughput.

Security/Compliance notes:
- SSO (SAML) and org-level audit logging required. KMS integration for secret handling. Data retention controls by customer.

POC plan and metrics to validate:
- Run dedicated perf matrix across three configs: Hosted baseline, Dedicated (2xA100 equivalent), Dedicated + quantized kernel.
- Measure: TTFT median/p95/p99, jitter p95/p99, bytes-per-token, server CPU/GPU utilization, partial-completion rate under backpressure.
- Acceptance criteria: median TTFT <70ms on warm for typical repo stimuli; p95 TTFT <120ms; jitter targets above. Session token budget signaling must hit client hooks in >=95% of tests.

Open items / asks for Redwood:
- Provide recommended streaming chunk config (chunk size vs frequency) and sample client backpressure API.
- Template for model pinning config and guidance on safe fallback chains.
- Cost estimate for Dedicated POC (2 nodes + burst to hosted) — see Drive pricing doc.

Next steps (short):
- 2026-03-10 — POC perf run (Ethan/Riley present) — evaluate Dedicated nodes.
- 2026-03-17 — Post-POC review and security checklist signoff.

Steno / CRM shorthand: TTFT SLOs/ jitter = gating; token-budget hooks = must-have; model pinning + graceful fallback = blocker until POC proven.

title: Cinderpath SaaSworks
preview:
Account background: Cinderpath is a mid-market B2B product analytics SaaS (user-facing product assistant + embedded code-assist feature). Currently using a major LLM API provider for chat/code assistant and embeddings. They have heavy token volume in user interactive chat (multi-turn) and periodic bulk reranking jobs for search.

Why Redwood: cost predictability at scale, better latency tail for their EU/NA customers, and desire for a single vendor that supports hosted then dedicated migration with minimal app changes. They asked specifically about Redwood's compat layer and prompt parity guarantees — worried about regressions when switching models/endpoints.

POC summary (Hosted API):
- Goals: 1) Verify prompt parity (no quality regressions), 2) Measure 95/99 latency and avg token cost, 3) Validate embedding similarity parity for product search.
- Setup: ported 3 canonical prompts + evaluation harness to Redwood Hosted endpoints; run A/B against existing provider over 48h window.
- Metrics being tracked: top1 ambiguity rate, hallucination incidents per 1k responses, avg tokens per conversation, 95th/99th latency, inference cost per 10k tokens.
- Early results (week 1): precision on rerank tests within 2% of baseline, embeddings cosine delta OK; avg latency down ~18% in NA, cost per 10k tokens ~12% lower on hosted config. 95/99 tails still under investigation for long-context API calls.

Technical asks from Cinderpath engineering:
- Redwood compat layer options (OpenAI-style endpoints) or light SDK shim to avoid rewriting client code.
- Controlled canary routing: start 5% traffic to Redwood Hosted during business hours only.
- Prompt parity tools: diff-run harness + automated regression alerts (they want fail-fast).
- KV cache persistence/behavior for chat state across retries and reconnects.
- Encryption/KMS and certs for Dedicated (timing for HSM integration important).

Commercial/Procurement notes:
- They prefer to start on Hosted with a short-term credit and then commit to Dedicated after a successful 3-month POC (expected Q2). Ask for a pricing schedule that maps to a 12-month reserved commit with burstable capacity.
- Procurement flagged need for a clear migration rollback plan and a definition of acceptable model divergence/regression SLA.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-20: Intro call - AE + SE - high level requirements captured (FF-20260225-7129).
- 2026-02-04: Demo of Hosted API + console; quick perf numbers shown.
- 2026-02-16: POC kickoff, shared evaluation harness and baseline dataset (Drive doc).
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire returned; asked for KMS and audit log samples (FF-20260218-6841).
- 2026-03-02: Mid-POC sync; embedding parity looks good, 95th latency variability noted in EU region.
- 2026-03-09: Pricing follow-up email from AE with 3 commit tiers; Cinderpath asked for a customized Dedicated estimate with specific GPU class and retention settings.

Quotes from stakeholders:
- "If prompt outputs diverge we lose product trust quickly — we need an automated regression gate before cutting traffic." — Head of Product.
- "Cost savings are meaningful, but uptime + predictable latency are why we'd move to Dedicated." — VP Eng.

Next steps (short):
- Deliver dedicated commitment pricing matrix by 2026-03-14.
- SE to run long-tail latency tests for long-context flows in EU region (end by 2026-03-20).
- Legal to provide redlined DPA + data residency clause; sync 2026-03-17.
- Schedule canary rollout plan review with CSM/Ops if POC OK.

Open blockers (notes):
- Need clear definition of "prompt parity" thresholds and acceptable delta for model outputs.
- Peering window: CIO requires VPC peering during maintenance window that currently conflicts with their nightly jobs.
- Legal: confirmation on subprocessors & EU data residency certification timeline.

Contact list: Ethan Park (AE), Maya Lopez (SE), Jordan Kim (CSM), CTO: Priya Raman (Cinderpath), Head of Product: Alex Mercer (Cinderpath).

title: Helion RAG Technologies
preview:
Summary: Helion is building an internal RAG-enabled search layer for several regulated product lines (investor docs, transaction records, compliance logs). Key adoption hinge is a seamless Vector DB connector and predictable unit economics for high QPS retrieval+rerank. Current stack: Postgres OLTP, Elastic for keyword indexes, small in-house pgvector PoC. They expect to scale to ~100M+ embedding vectors with mixed-size payloads (paragraphs + metadata). Primary goals: high recall for long-tail regulatory queries, sub-300ms p95 end-to-end for common flows, and cost predictability tied to token and compute usage.

Technical requirements observed on calls:
- Vector DB flexibility: must support hybrid search (sparse + dense), ANN index variants, and multi-region replicas. Preference ranking: Pinecone (managed), pgvector (self-hosted), Elastic (hybrid), Weaviate considered for schema embeddings.
- Connector requirements: high-throughput upserts (50k docs/day initial, burst to 500k), backfill/streaming ingestion support, metadata filtering at query time, consistent idempotent upserts, snapshot & restore, schema migration guidance.
- API expectations: single endpoint for retrieval that returns vector ids + distances and provides a hook for server-side reranker invocation. Redwood should supply an open connector spec (auth + streaming + backpressure) and example code for pgvector and Pinecone.
- Reranking workflow: prefer cross-encoder re-ranker hosted on Dedicated GPUs behind Redwood; rerank latency target p95 <= 200ms for top-10 candidates. Option for lightweight bi-encoder rerank on retrieval layer for latency-sensitive queries.
- Security & compliance: VPC peering or Private deployment required; KMS integration for key management and customer-controlled keys; full audit logs for vector ops, retention controls for embeddings + source docs, and guaranteed data residency in NA/EU shards.
- Observability: token-level cost breakdown, per-query latency trace, per-index cardinality, cold/hot shard monitoring, and eviction metrics for KV cache.

POC plan (agreed):
- Phase 0 (prep): Redwood to provide connector spec + ingestion runbook; Helion to provision sample dataset (20M paragraphs) and share schema.
- Phase 1 (ingest): Run ingestion to Pinecone and pgvector (Helion will spin up pgvector cluster in their VPC). Measure upsert throughput and index build times.
- Phase 2 (retrieval): Evaluate recall@10, latency (p50/p95), and cost per query across Redwood Hosted vs Dedicated. Test hybrid sparse+dense queries using Elastic + vector store combo.
- Phase 3 (rerank): Deploy Redwood-hosted cross-encoder reranker (smaller model + quantized option) and measure end-to-end latency. Iterate caching/batching config to meet p95 budget.
- Success metrics: recall@10 >= 0.82 on regulatory prompt set, rerank+retrieval p95 <= 250ms, cost per query <= $0.015 at projected QPS (200 qps sustained), connector upsert >= 50k/day sustained.

Quotes / call snippets:
- "We cannot accept unpredictable egress or hidden license fees — cost needs to be per-query and predictable." — Head of Search
- "If pgvector can meet latency and ops we prefer self-hosted; otherwise a managed Pinecone integration is acceptable with VPC peering." — CTO

Open questions / asks for Redwood:
- Provide a connector spec for pgvector and Pinecone (auth types, streaming upsert API, backpressure semantics).
- Confirm Dedicated latency SLOs for cross-encoder rerank per model family (e.g., small cross-encoders vs larger ones).
- Pricing scenario: monthly TCO breakdown for 200 qps, 120M vector index, and expected retention/replication settings.
- Security artifacts: KMS integration diagram, SOC2 attestation copy, and AES256-at-rest + TLS details.

Next steps (short): SE to send connector spec + example ingestion scripts by 2026-03-12. Schedule arch deep-dive 2026-03-18 with Helion infra + InfoSec. Legal to review SOC2 addendum and produce procurement checklist.

title: HelixScale Dynamics
preview:
Summary:
- Enterprise fintech customer evaluating Redwood Dedicated for latency-critical chat & reranking pipelines. Goal: replace homegrown infra with managed dedicated GPU pools while keeping strict compliance and residency controls.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2026-02-18: Capacity workshop (Priya + infra team). Whiteboard: baseline throughput and warm-pool strategy. (ff_20260218_capacity-workshop)
- 2026-02-26: Live demo of dedicated pool with H100 equiv. Ran synthetic load: 250 concurrent chat requests, avg seq len 256. Observed p95=140ms, p99=420ms for 1-replica warm.
- 2026-03-02: SLA/contract scoping call; legal asked for credit scheme for missed SLOs. (ff_20260302_helixscale_initial-demo)
- 2026-03-09: Follow-up on autoscaling policy proposals; sent draft sizing spreadsheet and cost model.

Use-case & perf targets (customer stated):
- Primary: customer-facing conversational agent (multi-turn, LLM-augmented). Secondary: document reranking for KYC workflows and embeddings for search.
- Latency SLOs: p95 <= 120ms for short-chat flows (<=128 tokens), p99 <= 350-450ms depending on model/context.
- Throughput: baseline sustained throughput ~1,200 tokens/sec across peak region, spike capacity for bursts to 3k tokens/sec. Typical concurrency target: 300 concurrent requests (avg seq 256).
- Availability: 99.95% monthly for live endpoints; 99.9% for maintenance windows acceptable with advanced notice.

Dedicated capacity requests / reserved GPU planning:
- Customer wants committed reserved capacity for baseline: initial ask = 8 x H100-class GPUs (dedicated) to meet 24/7 throughput.
- Warm standby: request for 2-4 hot standby GPUs (pre-warmed models/KV cache) to enable sub-1s scale-up.
- Burst: ability to burst to shared pool or cloud on-demand (up to +50% capacity) with contractual max egress/cost caps.
- Regional constraints: primary in us-east-1, must have EMEA replica for residency (smaller reserved pool there).

Autoscaling & capacity management concerns discussed (detailed):
- Scale-up triggers: prefer queue-depth + sustained CPU/GPU utilization thresholds. Proposed: scale-up when request queue depth > 40 for >30s OR GPU util > 70% for 60s.
- Scale-down policy: conservative hysteresis to avoid flapping. Proposed: scale-down only after 10m of sustained low utilization and step-down in max 1 GPU/step with cool-down 5m between steps.
- Warm pools: MUST maintain pre-loaded model weights + warmed KV cache for top-3 models. Warm pool size trade-offs discussed vs cost: agreed to experiment with 2 hot standbys initially.
- Pre-warming time: ~90-120s to reach fully warmed state for 70B-class models; for quantized 4-bit variants ~30-45s. Need precise catalog of model boot times per SKU.
- Queueing behavior: want per-tenant queue quotas and request prioritization (payment-tier priority for certain flows). Backpressure behavior: prefer 429 with Retry-After for overloads, but accept server-side queueing for short bursts.
- Batching strategy: use continuous batching for throughput economy but with max latency cap (do not batch >40ms for interactive chat).
- Fallbacks: automatic fallback to quantized variant or smaller model when capacity constrained, with header telling application which model used.

SLA negotiation points:
- Define p95/p99 by request-class (short-chat vs long context).
- Credits: linear crediting for missed monthly SLOs; customer pushing for larger credit bands until first-year adoption metrics are hit.
- Scheduled maintenance windows must be announced 72h in advance.
- Incident RTO/RPO expectations for dedicated pools and cross-region failover playbook.

POC milestones / plan (agreed):
- Phase 1 (2 weeks): baseline dedicated pool provision (8 GPUs) + synthetic perf tests mirroring production traffic.

Summary:
- Enterprise fintech customer evaluating Redwood Dedicated for latency-critical chat & reranking pipelines. Goal: replace homegrown infra with managed dedicated GPU pools while keeping strict compliance and residency controls.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2026-02-18: Capacity workshop (Priya + infra team). Whiteboard: baseline throughput and warm-pool strategy. (ff_20260218_capacity-workshop)
- 2026-02-26: Live demo of dedicated pool with H100 equiv. Ran synthetic load: 250 concurrent chat requests, avg seq len 256. Observed p95=140ms, p99=420ms for 1-replica warm.
- 2026-03-02: SLA/contract scoping call; legal asked for credit scheme for missed SLOs. (ff_20260302_helixscale_initial-demo)
- 2026-03-09: Follow-up on autoscaling policy proposals; sent draft sizing spreadsheet and cost model.

Use-case & perf targets (customer stated):
- Primary: customer-facing conversational agent (multi-turn, LLM-augmented). Secondary: document reranking for KYC workflows and embeddings for search.
- Latency SLOs: p95 <= 120ms for short-chat flows (<=128 tokens), p99 <= 350-450ms depending on model/context.
- Throughput: baseline sustained throughput ~1,200 tokens/sec across peak region, spike capacity for bursts to 3k tokens/sec. Typical concurrency target: 300 concurrent requests (avg seq 256).
- Availability: 99.95% monthly for live endpoints; 99.9% for maintenance windows acceptable with advanced notice.

Dedicated capacity requests / reserved GPU planning:
- Customer wants committed reserved capacity for baseline: initial ask = 8 x H100-class GPUs (dedicated) to meet 24/7 throughput.
- Warm standby: request for 2-4 hot standby GPUs (pre-warmed models/KV cache) to enable sub-1s scale-up.
- Burst: ability to burst to shared pool or cloud on-demand (up to +50% capacity) with contractual max egress/cost caps.
- Regional constraints: primary in us-east-1, must have EMEA replica for residency (smaller reserved pool there).

Autoscaling & capacity management concerns discussed (detailed):
- Scale-up triggers: prefer queue-depth + sustained CPU/GPU utilization thresholds. Proposed: scale-up when request queue depth > 40 for >30s OR GPU util > 70% for 60s.
- Scale-down policy: conservative hysteresis to avoid flapping. Proposed: scale-down only after 10m of sustained low utilization and step-down in max 1 GPU/step with cool-down 5m between steps.
- Warm pools: MUST maintain pre-loaded model weights + warmed KV cache for top-3 models. Warm pool size trade-offs discussed vs cost: agreed to experiment with 2 hot standbys initially.
- Pre-warming time: ~90-120s to reach fully warmed state for 70B-class models; for quantized 4-bit variants ~30-45s. Need precise catalog of model boot times per SKU.
- Queueing behavior: want per-tenant queue quotas and request prioritization (payment-tier priority for certain flows). Backpressure behavior: prefer 429 with Retry-After for overloads, but accept server-side queueing for short bursts.
- Batching strategy: use continuous batching for throughput economy but with max latency cap (do not batch >40ms for interactive chat).
- Fallbacks: automatic fallback to quantized variant or smaller model when capacity constrained, with header telling application which model used.

SLA negotiation points:
- Define p95/p99 by request-class (short-chat vs long context).
- Credits: linear crediting for missed monthly SLOs; customer pushing for larger credit bands until first-year adoption metrics are hit.

title: DiffLynx Labs
preview:
Account summary:
- Team: 120 engs, heavy focus on DX (internal code reviews) and third-party infra.
- Primary ask: low-latency PR review assistant that lives in VSCode + GitHub UI; must do diff summarization, generate inline comments, and provide a reranking API for comment suggestions.
- Perf targets: 95th pct end-to-end (< 300ms) for single-file diffs; for large multi-file diffs acceptable to batch but want tail latency guarantees for interactive use.
- Model requirements: ability to pin model versions per workspace; fast rollbacks; QA channel for nightly evals. Need quantization recommendations and suggestion to reduce cost for large-team usage.
- Integration notes: currently use Okta SSO, internal KMS for secrets, strict data residency (US only).
- POC scope: 2-week dedicated cluster (4x A100 equiv), integrate Redwood SDK into VSCode extension + GitHub PR webhook for server-side reranking. Measure: comment relevance (precision@3), false-positive rate, avg token cost.
- Customer quote: "We need deterministic, debuggable suggestions — not random hallucinations during PR review."
- Pricing sensitivity: mid-high; will pay for predictable capacity if SLA/routing works.
- Risks: legal wants subprocessor list; infra wants HSM/KMS pattern documented; eng team concerned about token costs on large monorepo.
- Suggested next actions: provide KMS/HSM integration docs, create a model pinning runbook, propose SLA for Dedicated (SLOs + fallbacks), schedule 1-week in-IDE POC with re-ranking telemetry capture.

title: Avenryx Platforms
preview:
Overview:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS (B2B), multi-tenant. Want to replace VendorX API for chat summarization + embeddings and move to Redwood Hosted first, then Dedicated once performance/cost targets validated.
- Primary ask: drop-in replacement for OpenAI-compatible flows, prompt parity, stable streaming, and identical behavior for stop sequences + function-calling.
Key non-functional requirements:
- Latency: 80th pctile < 350ms for 256-token chat requests in primary region.
- Throughput: burst to 70 rps for support-bot loads, steady 10–15 rps.
- Cost sensitivity: target <= 20% increase in monthly inference spend on Hosted during POC; Dedicated must show 30–50% unit cost reduction vs current committed pricing.
- Security: SSO (SAML), KMS integration, audit logs retained 1 year, EU data residency for customer EU tenants.
Migration concerns raised on calls:
- Tokenization differences: some prompts show longer token counts with Redwood model variants than VendorX; leads to marginal regressed outputs. Need tokenization parity check and recommended prompt rewrites.
- Function calling parity: VendorX handles nested JSON in tool responses differently; POC will include function-calling parity tests.
- Embeddings drift: small but measurable cosine distance shift on vendor switch for their fine-tuned embeddings. Want evaluation harness to compare retrieval hit-rate on canonical test set.
- Streaming behavior: partial delta ordering changes observed during demo; breaks client streaming parsers. SE to propose compatibility header or streaming mode toggle.
- Regeneration determinism: same prompt+seed returns slightly different summaries vs vendor. Investigate sampling/temperature mapping and top_p equivalences.
POC plan (agreed):
- Week 0: narrow scope, 10 prompts and 2 retrieval tasks chosen; share test harness (Drive link).
- Week 1: Redwood Hosted endpoints, run A/B vs VendorX on 50k tokens, collect metric set (latency, token usage, retrieval precision, user-acceptance).
- Week 2: Prompt parity fixes & rerun; escalate regressions to model/profile recommendations or warmed KV cache experiments.
- Post-POC: pricing conversation for Dedicated sizing (target: 4x T4 equivalent GPUs or Managed nv4 per capacity) and SLA.
Commercial notes:
- They are on a renewal with VendorX in Q3; looking to move before renewal if POC is clean.
- Procurement is sensitive to minimum commit for Dedicated; procurement wants a phased commitment with runway to expand.
Action items from last call (03/04):
- SE Ethan: provide tokenization parity checklist and lightweight shim examples for streaming compatibility (assigned, due 03/08).
- AE Maya: send initial Hosted pricing scenario + estimate for 2-week Hosted POC (sent 03/05).
- Security: Redwood security FAQ requested (Drive link).
- Avenryx to provide sample prompt set + reference retrieval corpus by 03/09.
Quotable lines from customer:
- \"We need a provider that won't surprise our product engineers — prompt changes can't silently change behavior in prod.\"
- \"If you can prove cost parity and fewer regressions, we'll consider Dedicated for next fiscal year.\"

title: Eudaimonia Health Co
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog 'HIPAA-adjacent best practices for SMBs' - CTO (Kevin Huang) signed up and ran quick PoC with free credits.

Context: Eudaimonia runs a small chain of therapy clinics + telehealth triage. They want a conversational intake assistant to help reduce front-desk time and auto-generate visit summaries for clinicians. Not planning to send PHI in initial integration but want to be able to handle semi-sensitive notes.

What they asked for / voiced concerns: 
- "We need a clear answer on whether Redwood Hosted API will ever use customer prompts for model training" (quoted paraphrase from Kevin).
- Retention controls: "Can we purge logs? set retention windows?"
- BAA: "Not immediate PHI, but want to know path if/when we need BAA".
- Residency: prefer US-only logs, may expand to Canada later.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, prefers predictable token pricing and quota alerts.

Quick wins suggested: share hosted FAQ + security one-pager, demo token-level cost breakdown in Console, show retention controls mockup. SE note: consider suggesting prefix-caching and short kv-cache TTLs for summaries to lower tokens.

Staging: discovery -> likely demo after SE session. Next: 30m technical call (wk of 2026-03-15) to validate retention flows and confirm no PHI in initial rollout.

Linked artifacts: fireflies ff:9b2a1c-2026-03-03 (call transcript), drive pricing + security docs.

CRM shorthand: inbound SMB, HIPAA-adjacent, public-cloud only for now, low-to-mid ARR, wants hosted_api, timeline: Q2 2026 decision if responsive.

title: Glacial Sprint Analytics
preview:
Top-level summary: trading-analytics firm building real-time signal and trade-assist features. Very latency sensitive (market-open spikes). Cost conscious — wants to avoid blowing budget on tail queries. Interest: Redwood Dedicated for predictable throughput + Private/VPC for data residency. Strong interest in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching profiles, caching rules, quantization tradeoffs).

Recent quote from CTO: "If p95 spikes over 50ms during US market open we lose confidence from desk users. We need predictable latency and a clear unit-cost story."

Key technical requirements (captured from demo):
- Latency target: p50 < 10ms for short lookups, p95 < 50ms for 512-token inferences; burst handling for 1.5-2x traffic during NYSE open.
- Throughput: baseline 400 req/s, target 2k req/s with autoscaling/burst.
- Cost target: lower per-token cost via batching & int8 quantization without >5% QA degradation.
- Routing/fallback: prefer regional routing (NA primary, EU DR), auto-fallback to smaller quantized variants if capacity constrained.
- Caching needs: prefix/KV cache for common market-context prompts (rolling 1min TTL), embedding cache for intra-day symbol vectors to avoid repeat recompute.
- Observability: token-level cost and traceable per-request latency breakdown; alerts when p95 increases >20% vs baseline.

POC scope agreed (two weeks):
- Week 1: baseline perf of current model on Redwood Hosted vs Dedicated. Measure raw latencies (cold/hot), token throughput, and failure modes. Create perf harness (JMeter + custom market-replay).
- Week 2: enable continuous batching, prefix KV caching, try int8 + 4-bit quantized variants; run A/B on quality vs cost. Redwood Optimize to propose batching profiles and quantization settings. Deliver final perf + cost report and recommended Dedicated pool size.

Timeline / recent activities:
- 2026-02-18: Intro call (AE Maya) — initial needs capture, CTO on call (ff_20260302 attached).
- 2026-02-25: Security kickoff; provided SOC2 docs and draft on-prem packaging plan (drive link).
- 2026-03-02: Demo — ran quick microbenchmarks; customer asked for p95 targets and quantization guidance. (fireflies id above).
- 2026-03-08: Internal pricing/architecture sync (AE + SE + Infra) — drafted Dedicated sizing.

Notes / CRM style fragments:
- Customer insists on proof of deterministic latency under burst (quantiles during 2x traffic).
- Wants explicit fallback plan: model->smaller-quant model->region-failover.
- "How do we guarantee cached prefix correctness during rolling market state?" SE notes: need cache invalidation policy tied to market ticks and event windows.
- Legal team: data residency clause for EU customers; want on-prem packaging for EU branch.
- Finance wants 12-month committed discount scenarios.

Open questions for Redwood team:
- Provide sample perf harness + example batching configs used in similar FIN workloads.
- Impact analysis: int8/4-bit quantization on top-K reranking quality (precision at K).
- SLA wording for Dedicated under market-open spikes.

Next steps (short):
- Ship POC agreement + access instructions (Maya).
- SE (Ethan) to provide initial batching/caching profile & recommended GPU type (H100 vs A100 tradeoffs).
- Schedule perf-run kickoff for 2026-03-15.

title: HarborLight DigitalWorks
preview:
CRM notes / call snippets:
- Product: mid-market product analytics SaaS; core feature: in-app contextual help + automated knowledge base answers.
- Primary goal: move from canned search to hybrid embed+rerank to reduce time-to-answer and deflection.
- Started with Hosted API to prototype. POC showed precision uplift ~18% with reranker.
- CTO (A. Verhoeven): "EU-only storage is a must for enterprise customers in our region."
- Team cares about latency - target p95 < 250ms for chat routing in EU; willing to accept slightly higher latency for archival workloads.
- Concern: model variants available in hosted region vs Dedicated GPUs; wants fallbacks if EU capacity constrained.
- Discussed multi-region routing: prefer EU primary, automatic failover to other regions only for non-sensitive workloads.
- Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions: batching profiles, quantization tradeoffs for embeddings.
- Security: need SSO/SAML integration, KMS key control and audit logging retained 2+ years.
- Pricing: procurement pushing for committed monthly spend vs pure on-demand. Looking for a 12-month committed offer to hit price targets.
- Quote request: Dedicated EU pool pricing + model throughput targets (tokens/s and embeddings qps).
- POC notes: payload patterns skew short-context (<=512 tokens) for chat + frequent tiny embeddings (128-d) bursts.
- Action items: send EU-region SLA wording, example routing policies, sample throughput numbers for n1-standard equivalent GPUs.
- Quote from Head of Product: "If latency in EU is within 200ms, we'll roll to beta with 5k users."

title: HighTide ML Platforms
preview:
Account: HighTide ML Platforms (platform for carrier routing + demand forecasting). Eng lead: CTO Maria Chen. Primary driver: predictable per-token cost and guaranteed latency for customer-facing chat and real-time reranking in routing UI. They have an internal SRE and a procurement timeline that pushes for signatures in Apr 2026.

Key points from discovery: 
- Want dedicated GPU pool (initial commit: 4x H100 equiv, scale to 12 within 3-6 months).
- Use cases: live chat for drivers (low-latency), reranking for route candidates (high throughput), nightly batch embedding compute for analytics.
- Strong requirement for VPC/private control plane + KMS integration. On-prem option for sensitive clients in EMEA.
- Cost sensitivity: wants clear unit economics per token / per-embedding and predictable monthly rate.

Quotes / call notes: 
- "We can't accept bursty cold-starts that kick users out of the UI" (Maria Chen).
- AE note: they requested a sample runbook for failover between regions.

SLA / legal interaction history: 
- Legal sent initial TS/PS SLA redlines 2026-02-28. 
- Redwood provided draft uptime table 2026-03-02. 
- Hightide countered with credit schedule 2026-03-05 (attached).

Other: pen-test required before go-live for any VPC/on-prem installs; schedule TBD.

Short sticky items: procurement wants 30-day invoice terms; security wants SOC2 plus explicit audit log retention 3 years.
